programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Extended Hours: PM
programs.description: This option provides supervised care after the end of the camp day for children registered in City of Toronto Parks, Forestry and Recreation camp. Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Workshop: Young Inventors
programs.description: Get ready to think, design, and build like a real inventor! In this hands-on workshop, participants will use a safe, kid-friendly vibrating saw to cut and shape cardboard into amazing creations. From imaginative sculptures to inventive gadgets, kids will learn how to plan, prototype, and bring their ideas to life.

programs.name: Music: Guitar
programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to guitar. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.description: Fun and fundamentals shape this introduction to guitar. Here you will learn proper hold, tuning, strumming and chords. As you continue to practice and learn how to read music you will work towards performing simple pieces.Equipment: You must bring your own guitar.

programs.name: Workshop: Hammered Flower Prints
programs.description: Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment? Hammered flower prints workshop, join us for a fun and educational workshop where you will Join us on Toronto Island to create colourful flower collage art,  in the enchanting Franklin Children's Garden. Pick your favourite flowers in  the garden and transfer their beautiful colours and shapes onto paper to make a  unique piece of art. Please arrive at the Jack Layton Ferry Terminal in time to take  the 9:00am ferry to Centre Island. Upon arrival at the Centre Island Ferry  Dock, our Garden Staff will meet you and accompany you on a short walk to  Franklin Children's Garden.

Join us for a hands-on adventure where crafting and playtime come together. Materials included.

programs.name: Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24). Learn comprehensive first aid training covering spinal injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the proper use of automated external defibrillators.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Workshop: Alcohol Ink Flow Art
programs.description: A vibrant, expressive workshop exploring alcohol inks on Yupo Paper. Participants learn flow techniques, bleeding and layering to create abstract pieces with bold colour movement.

programs.name: Zip, Seam & Stow
programs.description: Build your sewing skills while creating a series of stylish bags and purses in this hands-on workshop series. Designed for beginners and advancing sewists alike, this class will guide you through the process of making multiple bag styles - from simple origami bags to more structured designs with zippers, pockets and linings. Material fee of $10 (cash only) payable to instructor for practice materials. Material list will be provided.

programs.name: Workshop: Motor & Pre-Writing Development with Caregiver
programs.description: Join us for building skills that will help with future writing

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Workshop: Crafty Creations
programs.description: Join us for a summer craft

programs.name: Workshop: Watercolour - Dry-on-Dry
programs.description: Discover the precision and control of watercolour in this engaging program focused on the dry-on-dry technique! Participants will work with dry paper and a controlled brush to create crisp lines, defined shapes, and detailed compositions. Through guided exercises and creative projects, you’ll explore layering, texture-building, and fine brushwork while learning how to achieve depth and clarity in your artwork. Ideal for those looking to refine their technique or gain confidence with detail-oriented painting, this program offers a calm and supportive space to develop strong foundational skills and bring your artistic vision to life.

programs.name: Workshop: Watercolour - Dry-on-Dry
programs.description: Discover the precision and control of watercolour in this engaging program focused on the dry-on-dry technique! Participants will work with dry paper and a controlled brush to create crisp lines, defined shapes, and detailed compositions. Through guided exercises and creative projects, you’ll explore layering, texture-building, and fine brushwork while learning how to achieve depth and clarity in your artwork. Ideal for those looking to refine their technique or gain confidence with detail-oriented painting, this program offers a calm and supportive space to develop strong foundational skills and bring your artistic vision to life.

programs.name: Workshop: Youth Chocolate Art Lab
programs.description: A fun, experimental workshop where youth create edible art using melted chocolate, molds, textures and colour techniques. They design chocolate bars, lollipops and decorative pieces to take home

programs.name: Cyanotype Printing for Beginners
programs.description: Create one of a kind Cyanotype prints or photograms in this introductory photo printing workshop. Students will learn the step-by-step processes and methods needed to make these special prints. Explore the endless possibilities of imagery creation through experimentation with materials and found objects. This workshop requires an additional $20 material fee (cash only) payable to instructor. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Intro to Photoshop for Photography
programs.description: Take your images to the next level using Adobe Photoshop. Whether you're new to editing or looking to build a stronger foundation, this session will guide you through essential tools and techniques for enhancing your photos with confidence. Participants will learn how to make effective adjustments to exposure, colouur and contrast, as well as how to use layers, masks and basic retouching tools to refine images. Participants must already know how to import media from their cameras to their computer. Particiapnts are required to bring thier own images for editing during the workshop.

programs.name: Workshop: Natural Dyeing with Wool
programs.description: Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment? In this workshop, you will learn how to natural dye with plants and sheep's wool.

programs.name: Bronze Cross Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This course is for candidates looking to recertify their expired or expiring Bronze Cross certification. Participants will have the opportunity to review and update their techniques to maintain their current certification. Candidates will be required to perform the skills taught in Bronze Cross and must bring proof of original certification.Prerequisite: Bronze Cross and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Workshop: Young Inventors
programs.description: Get ready to think, design, and build like a real inventor! In this hands-on workshop, participants will use a safe, kid-friendly vibrating saw to cut and shape cardboard into amazing creations. From imaginative sculptures to inventive gadgets, kids will learn how to plan, prototype, and bring their ideas to life.

programs.name: Fusing Glass Workshop: Earrings
programs.description: This is your opportunity to design beautiful unique glass earrings just for you. In this workshop, you will make three pairs of dichroic glass earrings including two pairs of studs and one pair of dangle earrings. Please bring $30 cash to the class for materials. Fusing occurs off-site and pick up of works to be coordinated with the instructor.

programs.name: Etobicoke York Inclusive Request
programs.description: Our inclusive services are designed for participants who may require additional assistance to participate in recreation activities. Inclusive staff assist participants by providing modifications in program instruction, movement, equipment and communication. They also promote and encourage socialization amongst peers. Assignments of Inclusive staff are based on individual application and availability.As this is only a request, participants who waitlist for this activity will be contacted by Adapted and Inclusive Recreation Services for follow-up.
